zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn於1166年出生在西班牙時，他的父親Guillem II de Montcada11163歲，母親Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn16歲。他於1223年與Maragde de Narbonne在法國奧德納博訥結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1224年3月5日享年58歲，埋葬在法國阿奎泰培列內-阿特藍可斯奧盧洛-賽特-馬利奧盧洛-賽特-馬利-斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn于1166年出生在西班牙时，他的父亲Guillem II de Montcada11163岁，母亲Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn16岁。他于1223年与Maragde de Narbonne在法国奧德納博訥结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1224年3月5日享年58岁，埋葬在法国阿奎泰培列內-阿特藍可斯奧盧洛-賽特-馬利奧盧洛-賽特-馬利-斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Montcada
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Catalan: variant of Montcada, a habitational name from any of the places so named in Valencia and Catalonia.

nameMeanings.name: Bearn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

nickname for a bearded man (from Middle English berd, Old English beard). To be clean-shaven was the norm in non-Jewish communities in northwestern Europe from the 12th to the 16th century, the crucial period for surname formation. There is a placename and other evidence to show that this word was used as a byname in the Old English period, when beards were the norm; in this period the byname would have referred to a large or noticeable beard. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, in particular German Barth 1.

habitational name from a place called Beard in Derbyshire (now represented by Beard Hall and Beardwood Farms in New Mills parish), which derives its name by dissimilation from Old English brerd ‘rim, bank’.

variants.name: Bardwell
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bardwell in Suffolk, so named with an unattested Old English byname Bearda, a derivative of beard (see Beard ) + Old English well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Alternatively, the first element may be from a dissimilated form of Old English bre(o)rd ‘brim, bank’.

variants.name: Barth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Gunther, Klaus, Manfred, Armin, Arno.

German and Swiss German: nickname for a bearded man, from Middle High German bart ‘beard’. This name, which can not be always distinguished from 2 and 4 below, is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia, in the last four countries sometimes as a German translation of Slavic surnames meaning ‘beard’ or ‘bearded man’ (see Bart 1). See also Beard 1.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Pomerania.

variants.name: Bard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Aime, Alphonse, Alphy, Armand, Cecile, Celestine, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Jacques.

English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Old French personal name Bard(e), ancient Germanic Bardo, from barta ‘battle axe’. This was borne as a surname by a prominent Norman family with lands in west Normandy and in various English and Scottish counties, including Essex, Hertfordshire, North Yorkshire, Northumberland, and Lanarkshire. In Scotland the original family were Norman landowners in Strathaven parish in Lanarkshire. They were descended from the Baards, lords of Loftus in the North Yorkshire, descendants of the mid 12th-century Richard Baart.
Irish: altered form of either   Beard   or   Baird  .

variants.name: Bearb
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aurelien, Camille, Micheline, Pierre.
Altered form of English Beard , under the influence, in LA, of French barbe ‘beard’.

nameMeanings.name: Guillem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Ramón
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Raimundo . See Raymond .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn was born in 1166, in Spain, his father, Guillem II de Montcada, was 11163 and his mother, Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, was 16. He married Maragde de Narbonne in 1223, in Narbonne, Aude, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 5 March 1224, at the age of 58, and was buried in Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn est né en 1166, à Espagne, son père, Guillem II de Montcada, avait 11163 ans et sa mère, Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Maragde de Narbonne en 1223, à Narbonne, Aude, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 5 mars 1224, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn 1166 in Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillem II de Montcada 11163 und seine Mutter Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn 16. Er hat 1223 in Narbonne, Aude, Frankreich Maragde de Narbonne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 5. März 1224 im Alter von 58 Jahren und wurde in Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn è nato il nel 1166 nel Spagna, il suo padre, Guillem II de Montcada, aveva 11163 anni e il suo madre, Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Maragde de Narbonne nel 1223, nel Narbonne, Aude, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 5 marzo 1224, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearnが1166年にスペインで生まれた時，父Guillem II de Montcadaは11163歳，母Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarnは16歳であった。1223年にフランスアウドナルボンでMaragde de Narbonneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1224年3月5日に58歳で死去し，フランスアクイティアインプイルeンeエス-アトランティクスオロロン-サインテ-マリーオロロン-サインテ-マリー-エストにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn(이)가 1166년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillem II de Montcada(은)는 11163세였고, 그의 어머니 Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1223년 프랑스 오드 나르보니에서 Maragde de Narbonne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1224년 3월 5일 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 아키테인 p이ee스-앳랜티크스 로론-세인테-마이 로론-세인테-마이-스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn nasceu em 1166, em Espanha, seu pai, Guillem II de Montcada, tinha 11163 anos e sua mãe, Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Maragde de Narbonne em 1223, em Narbonne, Aude, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 5 de março de 1224, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn родился в 1166 г. в местности Испания, его отцу по имени Guillem II de Montcada было 11163 года, а матери, которую звали Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Maragde de Narbonne в 1223 г. в местности нарбонн, ауд, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 5 марта 1224 г. в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности олорон-сеинте-марие-ест, олорон-сеинте-мари, пирeнeес-атлантикуесь, аквитеин, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillem Ramón de Montcada y Bearn nació en 1166, en España, su papá, Guillem II de Montcada, tenía 11163 años y su mamá, Vicomtésse Marie de Gabarret-Guiscarda-Béarn, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Maragde de Narbonne en 1223, en Narbonne, Aude, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 5 de marzo de 1224, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Oloron-Sainte-Marie-Est, Oloron-Sainte-Marie, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Aquitaine, Francia.
